skill.name: quality-assessment
skill.description: Automates critical appraisal and quality assessment for research papers by analyzing text against established methodological standards (such as risk of bias tools, quality checklists, or reporting guidelines) and synthesizing a structured evaluation report. Use when you need to assess the methodological quality, internal validity, or reporting completeness of any type of study including RCTs, observational studies, systematic reviews, qualitative research, or diagnostic accuracy studies.
skill.summary: Automated research paper quality assessment using validated methodological standards and risk of bias tools.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 32 high-severity 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are false positives caused by research quality assessment tool names (ROBINS, QUADAS, PROBAST, AMSTAR) being misidentified as cryptographic primitives by the pattern scanner. All 23 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are false positives from Markdown backtick formatting in SKILL.md, not actual shell command execution. Two verified findings are legitimate expected behavior: a file write operation in extract_pdf.py for PDF text output, and a GitHub source URL citation. The skill is a legitimate research methodology tool with no malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Python file write operation
low_findings.description: extract_pdf.py writes extracted PDF text to a local output file. This is a legitimate and expected operation for the skills PDF text extraction functionality. No user data is transmitted externally.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed file write with open() for legitimate PDF text output purpose. No data exfiltration risk.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL reference
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains a GitHub URL citing the source repository. This is a legitimate source attribution and not a data exfiltration or network call.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URL points to the skills own GitHub source repository. Standard source citation practice.

content.user_title: Assess Research Paper Quality and Risk of Bias
content.value_statement: Evaluating research paper quality requires consistent application of established methodological standards across diverse study designs. This skill automates critical appraisal by analyzing text against validated quality assessment tools including RoB 2, ROBINS-I, QUADAS-2, and PROBAST, and synthesizes structured evaluation reports.
actual_capabilities: Identifies study design from research paper text to select the correct assessment framework
Recommends appropriate quality assessment tools based on study type from a library of over 40 validated tools
Extracts text from PDF research papers for structured analysis using PyPDF2
Applies established risk of bias frameworks including RoB 2, ROBINS-I, QUADAS-2, and PROBAST
Synthesizes structured quality assessment reports with domain-level bias ratings
Supports over 20 study types including RCTs, cohort studies, systematic reviews, and qualitative research
limitations: Cannot access paywalled or subscription-based research databases for full text retrieval
Quality assessment is limited to the text content available in the provided document
Automated assessment does not replace expert methodological judgment for complex or novel study designs
PDF text extraction accuracy depends on the source documents formatting and scan quality

use_cases.title: Systematic Review Risk of Bias Screening
use_cases.description: Screen included studies during a systematic review by applying validated risk of bias tools matched to each study design. Generate consistent bias assessments across all studies in the review.

use_cases.title: Manuscript Quality Evaluation for Peer Review
use_cases.description: Evaluate the methodological quality and reporting completeness of a submitted manuscript against established reporting guidelines and quality checklists.

use_cases.title: Research Methods Education and Training
use_cases.description: Demonstrate the application of quality assessment frameworks to sample research papers as a teaching tool for graduate students learning research methodology.
use_cases.target_user: Educators and graduate students in research methods

prompt_templates.title: Basic Quality Assessment Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess the quality of this research paper. First identify the study design, then select the appropriate quality assessment tool, and produce a structured evaluation report with bias ratings for each domain.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user needing a general quality assessment of a single research paper

prompt_templates.title: Tool-Specific Risk of Bias Evaluation
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate the risk of bias for this randomized controlled trial using the Cochrane RoB 2 tool. Assess each of the five domains: randomization process, deviations from intended interventions, missing outcome data, measurement of the outcome, and selection of the reported result.
prompt_templates.scenario: Experienced user who wants a specific tool framework applied to their study

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Study Quality Comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: I have three observational studies for my systematic review. Identify each study design and apply the appropriate quality assessment tool. Generate a comparison table showing strengths and limitations across all three studies.
prompt_templates.scenario: User conducting a systematic review with multiple studies to evaluate

prompt_templates.title: Full Text Extraction and Assessment Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract text from the provided PDF research paper using the extract_pdf.py script. After extraction, identify the study design, select the matching quality assessment tool, and produce a complete critical appraisal report with domain ratings and an overall quality judgment.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user running the complete workflow from PDF extraction through final assessment

output_examples.input: Assess the quality of this randomized controlled trial examining the effects of a new drug on blood pressure.
output_examples.output:
Study Design: Randomized Controlled Trial (RCT)

Selected Tool: Cochrane RoB 2 (Risk of Bias 2)

Domain Ratings:
1. Randomization process: Low Risk of Bias
2. Deviations from intended interventions: Low Risk of Bias
3. Missing outcome data: Some Concerns
4. Measurement of the outcome: Low Risk of Bias
5. Selection of the reported result: Low Risk of Bias

Overall Risk of Bias: Some Concerns

Summary: The study shows strong randomization and blinding procedures. Minor concerns arise from a small number of participants lost to follow-up without adequate sensitivity analysis.

output_examples.input: Evaluate the methodology of this cohort study investigating long-term outcomes of a surgical procedure.
output_examples.output:
Study Design: Cohort Study

Selected Tool: Newcastle-Ottawa Scale (NOS) for Cohort Studies

Domain Ratings:
Selection: 3 out of 4 stars
Comparability: 2 out of 2 stars
Outcome: 3 out of 3 stars

Total Score: 8 out of 9 stars

Assessment: High quality study. The cohort was representative of the population and the outcome assessment was adequate. The study controlled for the most important confounding factors.

best_practices: Confirm the study design before selecting a quality assessment tool to ensure the correct framework is applied
Review extracted text for completeness before running quality analysis to avoid missing critical methodological details
Document all assumptions and limitations in the final assessment report for transparency and reproducibility
anti_patterns: Applying quality assessment tools without first confirming the study design, which leads to incorrect framework selection
Using assessment scores to directly compare studies of different designs, which have different bias domains and criteria
Skipping the PDF text extraction validation step and assuming all content was captured correctly from the source document

faq.question: What study types does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill supports over 20 study types including randomized controlled trials, cohort studies, case-control studies, cross-sectional studies, systematic reviews, qualitative studies, diagnostic accuracy studies, and more.

faq.question: What quality assessment tools are included in the reference library?
faq.answer: The reference library includes over 40 validated tools such as RoB 2, ROBINS-I, QUADAS-2, PROBAST, AMSTAR-2, AGREE II, Newcastle-Ottawa Scale, and many more matched to specific study designs.

faq.question: Can this skill access paywalled research articles?
faq.answer: No. The skill requires the user to provide the full text of the research paper either as input text or as a PDF file. It cannot bypass paywalls or access subscription-based databases.

faq.question: How does the PDF text extraction process work?
faq.answer: The skill uses the extract_pdf.py script which leverages PyPDF2 to extract text content from PDF files. The extracted text is saved to a local file for subsequent quality assessment analysis.

faq.question: Can I compare quality across different study types?
faq.answer: Direct comparison across different study types is not recommended because each study design uses different quality assessment tools with distinct domains and scoring criteria.

faq.question: What output format should I expect from the quality assessment?
faq.answer: The skill produces a structured evaluation report with domain-level ratings, an overall quality judgment, and a summary of key methodological strengths and limitations.
